title: Physician Data Research (Sweet Dee)
detail:
Sweet Dee completed two major research tasks:
- `physician-data-market-research.md` — market landscape of physician data sources (free vs paid)
- `physician-data-technical-access.md` — technical access details for each source (API keys, endpoints, formats, rate limits)

Key finding: All core data sources are **free CMS datasets**. No paid sources under $1k/month were found.

---

title: Data Sources Explored (live data models pulled)
detail:
All sources join on **NPI** as primary key. Hospital data joins via **CCN**.

1. **NPPES NPI Registry** — free, no auth, bulk CSV + REST API. ~5M individual providers (NPI-1). Weekly incremental + monthly full.
2. **CMS Provider Data Catalog / National Downloadable File** (dataset: `mj5m-pzi6`) — free, CSV bulk only, quarterly. Physician + group practice profiles. Renamed from "Doctors and Clinicians."
3. **CMS Facility Affiliation Data** (dataset: `27ea-46a8`) — links physician NPIs to hospital CCNs. CSV, quarterly.
4. **Hospital Compare** (~50 datasets keyed on CCN) — Hospital General Info (`xubh-q36u`), HCAHPS (`dgck-syfz`), Readmissions (`9n3s-kdb3`), HAC (`yq43-i98g`), VBP (`ypbt-wvdk`). All free CSV.
5. **Medicare Part D Prescribers** — 3 files: by provider (`NPI.csv`), by provider+d

title: Token Usage — Today's Session
detail:
| Agent | Sessions | Tokens | Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| K2S0 (main) | 4 | 33.4M | $27.32 |
| Sweet Dee (research) | 7 (all deleted) | 1.32M | $3.15 |
| Dennis (pm) | 3 (2 deleted) | 50.7k | $0.16 |
| Charlie/Mac/Frank | 1 each (all deleted) | ~32k | $0.08 |
| **TOTAL** | | **34.8M** | **$30.71** |

Token composition: ~80% cache reads (cheap), ~12% cache writes, rest input/output.

**FIX NEEDED**: Always use `cleanup: "keep"` in `sessions_spawn` calls so token data persists for the dashboard.

title: Voice Interface - Duplex Working
detail:
- Built full duplex voice interface: mic → Whisper → Discord (as Sean via user token) → K2S0 replies → local OpenAI TTS → afplay
- Key file: ~/.openclaw/workspace/voice_interface/voice_interface.py (v2)
- Required env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_USER_TOKEN, K2S0_BOT_ID
- DISCORD_USER_TOKEN is Sean's personal Discord token (sends messages as icky_thump_405)
- Background watcher thread monitors channel every 2.5s for K2S0 replies and plays via afplay
- Voice threshold calibrates at startup (ambient * 1.3); Sean needs to speak loudly due to background noise
- Run command: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace/voice_interface && source venv/bin/activate && set -a && source ~/.openclaw/.env && set +a && python voice_interface.py

---

title: Voice Interface - Additional Details (evening session)
detail:
### Architecture (final working v2)
- `voice_interface.py` sends messages using **DISCORD_USER_TOKEN** (Sean's personal token, not bot token)
  - Messages appear as `icky_thump_405` → OpenClaw routes them to K2S0 agent
  - Bot token posts as K2S0 → OpenClaw ignores (self-filter) — this was the core bug
- Background watcher thread polls every 2.5s for K2S0 bot messages after a seeded message ID
- No per-message reply tracking — just a global channel monitor
- TTS: OpenAI `tts-1` model, voice `onyx` (switched from `nova` per Sean's request)
- Robot voice effect: sox pitch -200 + chorus + reverb (K-2SO style)
- sox installed via brew install sox

### Tokens stored in ~/.openclaw/.env
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` — for Whisper STT + TTS
- `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` — K2S0 bot token (for reading channel message

title: Voice Interface Progress
detail:

- **CONFIRMED WORKING**: Sean heard weather forecast through speakers ✅

title: What I Built
detail:
### Task 1: Model + Token fields in agent dashboard
- Added `model`, `model_short`, `estimated_tokens`, `workspace_chars` to `/agents` endpoint
- Reads model from `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (all agents using global default `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`)
- Token estimate = workspace chars / 4 (rough but functional)
- Dashboard agent cards now show 🧠 model name and 🔢 estimated tokens

### Task 2: Enriched Activity Feed
- Rewrote `detect_file_changes()` to capture snippet (last 5 lines), size delta, line delta, event_detail string, severity
- Added `read_file_snippet()` and `count_file_lines()` helpers
- Added `file_size_cache` and `file_linecount_cache` for delta tracking
- Boot events emitted per agent on server startup
- Idle events fire after 10+ min of silence (resets so no spam)
- Seve

title: Decisions Made
detail:
- Newest-at-bottom for feed (more natural for a log-style view)
- Idle event only fires once per idle transition (cleared after emit to avoid spam)
- Size delta shown as `+Xb` / `-Xb` with green/red coloring
- Server pushes enriched events via `/activity` endpoint; dashboard fetches both `/agents` and `/activity`

title: Session Summary
detail: Completed research task on the **Nuremberg Celestial Phenomenon of 1561**.

title: Report Filed
detail: `reports/nuremberg-1561-celestial-phenomenon.md`

title: Key Findings
detail:
- Event: April 14, 1561, dawn, Nuremberg (Holy Roman Empire)
- Primary source: Hans Glaser broadsheet/woodcut, same month as event
- Original document archived at Zentralbibliothek Zürich
- Describes blood-red spheres, cylinders, crosses, rods in aerial "battle" lasting 1+ hour; followed by black spear-shaped object
- Cultural context: Reformation-era prodigy broadsheet tradition — religious warning genre
- Scientific theories: sun dogs (parhelia) most cited; also meteor activity; likely multi-cause
- Modern connection: Carl Jung cited it in *Flying Saucers* (1958) — psychological/collective unconscious interpretation
- Comparable event: 1566 Basel celestial phenomenon (similar description, same broadsheet genre)
- UFO interpretation: anachronistic; broadsheet was a theological pamphlet, n

title: Sources Worth Remembering
detail:
- Public Domain Review (publicdomainreview.org) — high quality cultural history writing, good images
- Zentralbibliothek Zürich / e-manuscripta.ch — original archival documents online
- Swiss National Museum blog — solid academic history of the Basel 1566 event
- Jung's *Flying Saucers* (1958) — still in print, Princeton/Bollingen

---

title: Session 2: Physician Data Market Research
detail:
**Report Filed:** `reports/physician-data-market-research.md`

### Key Findings

**Free / Public datasets (CMS):**
- NPPES NPI Registry — full physician universe, addresses, specialties (~7M NPIs)
- Medicare Part D Prescriber Data — Rx volume by prescriber + drug (Medicare only, annual)
- Medicare Part B Utilization — procedure volumes by physician (annual)
- Open Payments (Sunshine Act) — pharmaceutical payments to physicians, ~$2B+/year reported
- Care Compare / Provider Data Catalog — quality metrics, affiliations, board certifications

**AMA Physician Masterfile (licensed):**
- ~1.4M records including inactive/residents
- Includes DEA numbers, board certifications, training history, practice setting
- The gold standard private physician database — anchors most pharma targeting programs

title: Notes
detail: The Nuremberg broadsheet is a genuinely fascinating document regardless of what was in the sky. It sits at the intersection of early print media, Reformation anxiety, atmospheric science, and the history of anomalous phenomena reporting. Good topic.

title: Task: AI Design Tools Research for Cricket
detail:
Completed comprehensive research report on AI design tools for Cricket (K2S0 designer agent).

### Report files:
- `reports/ai-design-tools-research.md`
- `reports/ai-design-tools-research.html`

### Tools Covered:

**Image Gen:** Midjourney, DALL-E/GPT-Image-1, Adobe Firefly, Ideogram

**UI/UX:** Figma AI, Galileo AI, Uizard, Visily

**General Design:** Canva AI (Magic Studio), Adobe Express, Looka

### Recommended Stack for Cricket:
1. **Figma + Figma AI** — primary design environment (free → $16/mo)
2. **Galileo AI Standard** — rapid UI generation from prompts ($16/mo, exports to Figma)
3. **Ideogram** — image/asset gen with API (free → $20/mo Plus)

Total: ~$52/mo fully loaded

- Midjourney has NO official API — only third-party unofficial wrappers
